CHAPTER 12 — USING EXCEL IN ACCOUNTING AND FINANCIAL REPORTING

Money changes value over time. This is not a theory. It is written into every interest rate, every loan contract, every

Chapter 12 — Using Excel in Accounting a — All time-value-of-money calculations rest on five variables. Excel has isolated them and made each into a function. Know
NARRATION

Money changes value over time. This is not a theory. It is written into every interest rate, every loan contract, every investment decision. A dollar in your hand today is worth more than a dollar next year, because today you can put it to work , invest it, lend it, spend it and avoid borrowing later. Wait a year, and that possibility is gone. All time-value-of-money calculations rest on five variables. Excel has isolated them and made each into a function.

// SLIDE 02 — NET PRESENT VALUE: WHY A DOLLA

NET PRESENT VALUE: WHY A DOLLAR IN YEAR FIVE IS NOT A DOLLAR TODAY

—
What Question It Answers — What Question It Answers — first option applied to the chapter example.
How To Interpret The Output — How To Interpret The Output — first option applied to the chapter example.
When The Two Methods Agree — When The Two Methods Agree — first option applied to the chapter example.
NARRATION

A company is considering two pieces of equipment. Both require money today and generate cash over four years. Which is the better investment? You cannot compare them by simply adding up the cash flows and comparing totals. A dollar received in year four is worth less than a dollar received in year one, because the year-one dollar has three more years to compound. To compare cash flows at different times, you have to discount them back to a common moment , today.

// SLIDE 03 — DEPRECIATION: THE MATHEMATICS

DEPRECIATION: THE MATHEMATICS OF ASSET DECAY

—
Side-By-Side Depreciation Schedule Comparing SLN — Side-By-Side Depreciation Schedule Comparing SLN — year applied to th
DB For A $50,000 Asset With $5,000 Salvage Value — DB For A $50,000 Asset With $5,000 Salvage Value — year applied to th
5-Year Life — — 5-Year Life — — year applied to the chapter example.
NARRATION

Equipment loses value as you use it. The tax code recognizes this and allows you to deduct that loss as a business expense, reducing taxable income each year over the asset's life. Excel automates the arithmetic. Three variables govern every depreciation calculation:

// SLIDE 04 — FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AS FORMUL

FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AS FORMULA SYSTEMS.

25 percent
annual growth, year-two revenue is:
→
40 percent
of revenue, is:
→
20 percent
instead of 25
NARRATION

A financial statement in Excel is a system of formulas, not a list of numbers. This is the design principle that separates a model you can trust from a model that breaks silently.

// SLIDE 05 — THE HARD LINE BETWEEN EXCEL AN

THE HARD LINE BETWEEN EXCEL AND ACCOUNTING SOFTWARE

Excel is for analysis and modeling. It is not a ledger.

The Hard Line Between Excel and Accounti — This distinction is not a preference. It is structural. Accounting software — QuickBooks, SAP, Oracle — enforces the rul
NARRATION

Excel is for analysis and modeling. It is not a ledger. This distinction is not a preference. It is structural. Accounting software , QuickBooks, SAP, Oracle , enforces the rules of double-entry bookkeeping: every transaction is recorded once and only once, debits and credits always balance, the chart of accounts stays consistent across periods. When you enter a transaction, the software enforces these constraints.

// SLIDE 06 — WHAT VERIFICATION ACTUALLY LOO

WHAT VERIFICATION ACTUALLY LOOKS LIKE.

Every concept in this chapter has a verification habit attached to it. This is not optional overhead. It is the work.

What Verification Actually Looks Like — For time-value-of-money functions: check the units. The interest rate and the number of periods must be in the same time
NARRATION

Every concept in this chapter has a verification habit attached to it. This is not optional overhead. It is the work. For time-value-of-money functions: check the units. The interest rate and the number of periods must be in the same time unit. An annual rate with monthly periods gives a payment that is far too low. Run the plausibility check: is the output higher than the principal divided by the number of periods? If not, something is wrong.

// SLIDE 07 — EXERCISES

EXERCISES

6 percent
annual interest over 4 years
→
7 percent
discount rate
→
8 percent
discount rate
NARRATION

A company wants to finance a $180,000 piece of equipment at 6 percent annual interest over 4 years. Write the PMT formula to calculate the annual payment. Then rewrite it to calculate the monthly payment. What two things change between the annual and monthly versions, and why must they both change together? Tests: the unit-alignment rule , RATE and NPER must be in the same time unit, and changing one without the other produces a nonsense answer. Difficulty: low.

// SLIDE 08 — AI WAYBACK MACHINE

AI WAYBACK MACHINE.

Ask it to explain how the debit/credit logic of 1494 maps onto a modern Excel income statement and balance sheetwhat's the same, what's different, what's better, what's worse
Ask"Pacioli was a Franciscan friar, a mathematics teacher, and a friend of Leonardo da Vinci. How did each of those roles s
Add the framing"Answer as if you're a Venetian merchant in 1500 explaining why your apprentice should learn this new bookkeeping method
NARRATION

The ideas in this chapter didn't appear from nowhere. Luca Pacioli was working on Summa de Arithmetica (1494) , the first published description of double-entry bookkeeping, the accounting method every modern Excel financial model still uses decades before most people had heard of accounting and financial reporting in spreadsheets. Here's a prompt to find out more , and then make it better.

// SLIDE 09 — PROMPTS

PROMPTS

Create a single-file D3 v7 HTML process using 4-6 labeled nodes or panels, directional connectors where sequence matters

Prompts — Create a single-file D3 v7 HTML process using 4-6 labeled nodes or panels, directional connectors where sequence matters
NARRATION

Create a single-file D3 v7 HTML process using 4-6 labeled nodes or panels, directional connectors where sequence matters, direct labels on every mark, one primary red emphasis mark, neutral supporting marks, and no unlabeled boxes. The figure should represent: a five-year income statement flow diagram showing how the growth rate assumption feeds into revenue in each year, and how revenue feeds into COGS, gross profit, operating profit, and net profit , with arrows showing the formula dependencies, making visible which cells are inputs and which are calculations.
